title: Full observability architecture
prompt: Design the observability stack for our microservices platform
expected_behavior: Produces all 6 sections: strategy with OTel Collector topology (hierarchical agent+gateway), structured logging with mandatory fields, distributed tracing with W3C propagation, RED/USE metrics with naming conventions, SLO-based burn rate alerting, and incident response integration.

title: SLO-based alerting design
prompt: Set up alerting for our services — we're drowning in false alerts
expected_behavior: Focuses on S5: replaces threshold alerting with SLO-based burn rate model (14.4x fast, 6x medium, 1x slow), multi-window approach, severity levels (P1-P3), mandatory runbook linkage, and alert hygiene metrics.

title: Observability for legacy monolith
prompt: Add monitoring to our legacy monolith — it only has access logs today
expected_behavior: Addresses the edge case: starts with infrastructure metrics and access logs, adds structured logging incrementally, uses APM agents for automatic instrumentation, traces at external call boundaries.

title: Cardinality explosion management
prompt: Our Prometheus storage costs are exploding because of too many time series
expected_behavior: Covers S4 cardinality management: normalize URL paths with IDs, remove per-user labels, allowlist known enum values, enforce per-service series budgets (max 5000), use delta temporality for high-cardinality counters.

title: False positive — load testing request
prompt: Run load tests against our API to find the breaking point
expected_behavior: Does NOT activate this skill. Recognizes this is performance engineering / load testing, not observability architecture design. Defers to performance-engineering skill.
